description:
# Delete the closure_lit-driven return LFP machinery

## Goal

`compute_effective_returns` (in `src/ir_typer.rs`) currently includes
a closure_lit-driven branch for `Term::TailCallClosure` that walks
each lit clause's resolved body and joins the return.
`resolve_closure_return` is the matching helper.

After the reducer has resolved closure_lits at compile time (RED.5),
these LFP branches are dead. Delete them.

## Specifics to remove

- The `Term::TailCallClosure` closure_lit branch in
  `compute_effective_returns`.
- `resolve_closure_return` (if no other live caller remains; check
  `cont_slot0_descr` and `walk_spec_for_discovery`).
- Related helpers if exclusively consumed.

## Acceptance

- All goldens byte-identical to RED.6's blessed state.
- Cargo test green.
- LOC reduction: ~50-100 lines from `ir_typer.rs`.

## Removals

This **is** the removal ticket.

comments.text: Also remove the closure_lit branch in ir_codegen.rs::cont_slot0_descr (the closure_d.arrow_join_return() path). After RED.5 dissolves resolvable closure_lits, the surviving CallClosure sites are genuinely opaque and this branch is dead too.

Superseded by fz-9pr (callsite outcomes). The .8a/b/c removals were predicated on the closure_lit branch being dead — that's not the right framing. The new epic reframes: those branches become OUTPUTS of a unified callsite-outcome producer (the reducer-with-Inlined), and the typer's discovery walk shrinks because outcomes are first-class data on the Module. See docs/callsite-outcomes.md.

title: fz-RED.8b REMOVE: closure_lit return chain in compute_effective_returns
